question: Write a C++ function `int linearSearch(int arr[], int size, int target)` that performs a linear search on a 1D integer array. It should return the index of the `target` element if found, or -1 if not found. Test this function in `main` with a sample array and target values.
errors: The loop condition in linearSearch is `i <= size` instead of `i < size`. This causes an out-of-bounds access when `i` equals `size`, attempting to read `arr[size]`. This is undefined behavior and can lead to incorrect results or crashes, especially when the target is not found or when `size` is 0.
